author: Alexander of Alexandria
book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: VI.—The Addition in the Codex, with a Various Reading.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: God, therefore, wishing to visit His own form which He had fashioned after His own image and similitude, hath in these last times sent into the world His incorporeal and only Son, who being in the Virgin’s womb incarnate, was born perfect man to raise erect lost man, re-collecting His scattered members.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: For why else should Christ have died?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Was He capitally accused?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: And since He was God, why was He made man?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Why did He who was reigning in heaven come down to earth?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Who compelled God to come down to earth, to take flesh of the holy Virgin, to be wrapped in swaddling clothes and laid in a manger, to be nourished with milk, to be baptized in the Jordan, to be mocked of the people, to be nailed to the tree, to be buried in the bosom of the earth, and the third day to rise again from the dead; in the cause of redemption to give life for life, blood for blood, to undergo death for death?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: For Christ, by dying, hath discharged the debt of death to which man was obnoxious.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Oh, the new and ineffable mystery! the Judge was judged.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: He who absolves from sin was bound; He was mocked who once framed the world; He was stretched upon the cross who stretched out the heavens; He was fed with gall who gave the manna to be bread; He died who gives life.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: He was given up to the tomb who raises the dead.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: The powers were astonished, the angels wondered, the elements trembled, the whole created universe was shaken, the earth quaked, and its foundations rocked; the sun fled away, the elements were subverted, the light of day receded; because they could not bear to look upon their crucified Lord.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: The creature, in amazement, said, What is this novel mystery?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: The judge is judged and is silent; the invisible is seen and is not confounded; the incomprehensible is grasped and is not indignant at it; the immeasurable is contained in a measure and makes no opposition; the impassable suffers and does not avenge its own injury; the immortal dies and complains not; the celestial is buried and bears it with an equal mind.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: What, I say, is this mystery?

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: The creature surely is transfixed with amazement.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: But when our Lord rose from death and trampled it down, when He bound the strong man and set man free, then every creature wondered at the Judge who for Adam’s sake was judged, at the invisible being seen, at the impassable suffering, at the immortal dead, at the celestial buried in the earth.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: For our Lord was made man; He was condemned that He might impart compassion; He was bound that He might set free; He was apprehended that He might liberate; He suffered that He might heal our sufferings; He died to restore life to us; He was buried to raise us up.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: For when our Lord suffered, His humanity suffered, that which He had like unto man; and He dissolves the sufferings of him who is His like, and by dying He hath destroyed death.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: It was for this cause that He came down upon earth, that by pursuing death He might kill the rebel that slew men.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: For one underwent the judgment, and myriads were set free; one was buried, and myriads rose again.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: He is the Mediator between God and man; He is the resurrection and the salvation of all; He is the Guide of the erring, the Shepherd of men who have been set free, the life of the dead, the charioteer of the cherubim, the standard-bearer of the angels, and the King of kings, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
